_doc: The forge-issue verb map — the ONE home for 'how do I talk to each forge's issues'. PERSONA-READ REFERENCE: unlike deny-rules.json and tool-map.json (loaded at runtime by the deny engine), NO code loads this file. The orchestrator READS it (its `## Backlog` procedure) when a project sets `collaboration.backlog: forge`, to find the right CLI verb for the resolved `collaboration.forge`. It is data so it stays one structured home (no verb copied into prose), and so a shape test (src/adapter/forge-map.test.mjs) can guard it from rotting — but it carries no predicate and changes nothing mechanically. The neutral act is 'record / read a backlog item'; `backlog: file` realises it as an edit of .ai-dev/backlog.md, `backlog: forge` realises it as one of the issue operations below. Forge auth (gh/glab/tea tokens) is the Operator's environment — this map names CLI verbs only, never a credential.
_operations: The five issue operations the backlog adapter needs: create · list · view · close · comment. Common flag vocabulary (one abstraction across all three forges): title, body/description, label, assignee, milestone. Each forge row maps that vocabulary to its own CLI. Where a forge's exact flag was not verified against its docs, the operation + canonical subcommand are given and the uncertain flag is marked `[unverified]` — never fabricated; confirm with `<cli> issue <op> --help`.
_auto_detect: Resolving `collaboration.forge: auto` ⇒ inspect the `origin` remote host: host github.com ⇒ github; gitlab.com ⇒ gitlab; a self-hosted host has no canonical signature (a private GitLab or Gitea looks like any host) ⇒ NOT auto-resolvable — the orchestrator confirms the forge with the Operator (or probes the remote's API banner). Unresolved ⇒ fail safe to `backlog: file` (orchestrator's `## Backlog`), never guess a forge.
_issue_pr_link: On all three forges, `closes #N` / `fixes #N` in the PR body auto-closes the linked issue on merge — the same close-on-ship convention regardless of forge.

flags.title: -t/--title
flags.body: -b/--body (or -F/--body-file - for stdin — the agent's non-interactive path)
flags.label: -l/--label
-l/--label
flags.state: no single --state flag — open is the default; --all gets all, -c/--closed gets only closed

gitea.auto_host: self-hosted (no canonical public host — confirm with the Operator)

flags.note: no `view` subcommand — a bare index shows the issue (`tea issues` lists, `tea issue <index>` shows one)
comment is a SEPARATE top-level command (`tea comment` / `tea c`), NOT `tea issue comment`
